verses.text: The remnant comes to Jeremiah, begging him to pray that God would show them where to go. They seek guidance. It is good to ask God's direction before taking a great step.
The remnant comes to Jeremiah, begging him to pray that God would show them where to go. They seek guidance. It is good to ask God's direction before taking a great step.
The remnant comes to Jeremiah, begging him to pray that God would show them where to go. They seek guidance. It is good to ask God's direction before taking a great step.
Jeremiah agrees, and they vow to obey whatever God says, good or evil. A solemn promise. Yet a vow is easily made and harder kept, as their hearts would soon prove.
Jeremiah agrees, and they vow to obey whatever God says, good or evil. A solemn promise. Yet a vow is easily made and harder kept, as their hearts would soon prove.
Jeremiah agrees, and they vow to obey whatever God says, good or evil. A solemn promise. Yet a vow is easily made and harder kept, as their hearts would soon prove.
After ten days the word comes: stay in the land, and I will build you, 'for I repent me of the evil that I have done.' Mercy waits. God offers to settle and bless them if they will only trust Him.

After ten days the word comes: stay in the land, and I will build you, 'for I repent me of the evil that I have done.' Mercy waits. God offers to settle and bless them if they will only trust Him.
'Be not afraid of the king of Babylon... for I am with you to save you.' His presence is the cure for fear. Where God promises to be with us, dread of man is groundless.
'Be not afraid of the king of Babylon... for I am with you to save you.' His presence is the cure for fear. Where God promises to be with us, dread of man is groundless.
But if they flee to Egypt to escape sword and famine, the very things they fear shall overtake them there. Flight from God's will is folly. We cannot outrun the troubles we disobey to avoid.

As His fury fell on Jerusalem, so it will fall on them in Egypt; He warns they 'dissembled' in their hearts. Their asking was insincere. God reads the heart that seeks counsel only to do its own will.
